compiled_state.title: [RED] Write failing integration test for ticket check-ac dispatcher
compiled_state.author: Worktree
compiled_state.description:
## Description

Write a failing RED integration test for the `ticket check-ac` subcommand before the implementation exists.

## File Impact
- tests/scripts/test-ticket-check-ac-dispatcher.sh (new)
- .test-index (modify — append to `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket` and `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-lib-api.sh` entries)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/scripts/test-ticket-check-ac-dispatcher.sh exists and is executable (chmod +x)
- [ ] Test invokes `ticket check-ac <id>` through the dispatcher (not check-acceptance-criteria.sh directly)
- [ ] Test uses a mock TICKET_CMD returning fixture ticket JSON with an ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA section
- [ ] Test asserts exit 0 and "AC_CHECK: pass" output when AC block is present
- [ ] Test asserts exit 1 and "AC_CHECK: fail" output when AC block is absent
- [ ] Tests FAIL (RED) before implementation task T2 runs
- [ ] .test-index updated to add test file to both dispatcher and lib entries

## Red Marker
tests/scripts/test-ticket-check-ac-dispatcher.sh [test_check_ac_routes_through_dispatcher]

## Notes
check-acceptance-criteria.sh uses TICKET_CMD to call `ticket show <id>`. The mock TICKET_CMD should return fixture JSON with a description containing "## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA" for the pass case and no such section for the fail case. Exit codes: 0=pass, 1=fail (AC block missing or ticket not found).
